name: tmdbv3api

metadata.summary: A lightweight Python library for The Movie Database (TMDb) API.

documentation:
# tmdbv3api

```{image} https://travis-ci.org/AnthonyBloomer/tmdbv3api.svg?branch=master
:alt: Build Status
:target: https://travis-ci.org/AnthonyBloomer/tmdbv3api
```

```{image} https://codecov.io/gh/AnthonyBloomer/tmdbv3api/branch/master/graph/badge.svg
:alt: codecov
:target: https://codecov.io/gh/AnthonyBloomer/tmdbv3api
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/AnthonyBloomer/tmdbv3api
:alt: GitHub release (latest by date)
:target: https://github.com/AnthonyBloomer/tmdbv3api/releases
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/tmdbv3api
:alt: PyPI
:target: https://pypi.org/project/tmdbv3api/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/tmdbv3api.svg
:alt: Downloads
:target: https://pypi.org/project/tmdbv3api/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/github/commits-since/AnthonyBloomer/tmdbv3api/latest
:alt: GitHub commits since latest release (by date) for a branch
:target: https://github.com/AnthonyBloomer/tmdbv3api/commits/master
```

A lightweight Python library for The Movie Database (TMDb) API. The TMDb API is a resource for developers to integrate movie, TV show and cast data along with posters or movie fan art. themoviedb.org is a free and community edited database.

Register an account: <https://www.themoviedb.org/account/signup>

Check out the API documentation: <https://developers.themoviedb.org/3/getting-started/introduction>

## Install

tmdbv3api is available on the Python Package Index (PyPI) at
<https://pypi.python.org/pypi/tmdbv3api>

You can install tmdbv3api using pip.

```
$ pip install tmdbv3api
```

## Usage

The first step is to initialize a TMDb object and set your API Key.

```python
from tmdbv3api import TMDb
tmdb = TMDb()
tmdb.api_key = 'YOUR_API_KEY'
```

Alternatively, you can export your API key as an environment variable.

```bash
$ export TMDB_API_KEY='YOUR_API_KEY'
```

Other configuration settings include defining your `language` and enabling `debug` mode, for example:

```python
tmdb.language = 'en'
tmdb.debug = True
```

Then to communicate with TMDb, create an instance of one of the objects
and call that instances methods. For example, to retrieve movie
recommendations for a given movie id:

```python
from tmdbv3api import Movie

movie = Movie()

recommendations = movie.recommendations(movie_id=111)

for recommendation in recommendations:
    print(recommendation.title)
    print(recommendation.overview)
```

## Examples

Get the list of popular movies on The Movie Database. This list
refreshes every day.

```python
movie = Movie()
popular = movie.popular()

for p in popular:
    print(p.id)
    print(p.title)
    print(p.overview)
    print(p.poster_path)
```

Get the primary information about a movie.

```python
m = movie.details(343611)

print(m.title)
print(m.overview)
print(m.popularity)
```

Search for movies by title.

```python
search = movie.search('Mad Max')

for res in search:
    print(res.id)
    print(res.title)
    print(res.overview)
    print(res.poster_path)
    print(res.vote_average)
```

Get the similar movies for a specific movie id.

```python
similar = movie.similar(777)

for result in similar:
    print(result.title)
    print(result.overview)
```

Search for TV shows by title.

```python
tv = TV()
show = tv.search('Breaking Bad')

for result in show:
    print(result.name)
    print(result.overview)
```

Get the similar TV shows for a specific tv id.

```python
similar = tv.similar(1396)

for show in similar:
    print(show.name)
    print(show.overview)
```

Get the details of TV season for a specific tv id.

```python
season = Season()
show_season = season.details(1396, 1)
print(show_season.air_date)
print(len(show_season.episodes))
```

Get the general person information for a specific id.

```python
person = Person()
p = person.details(12)

print(p.name)
print(p.biography)
```

Discover movies by different types of data like average rating, number
of votes, genres and certifications.

```python
# What movies are in theatres?

discover = Discover()
movie = discover.discover_movies({
    'primary_release_date.gte': '2017-01-20',
    'primary_release_date.lte': '2017-01-25'
})

# What are the most popular movies?

movie = discover.discover_movies({
    'sort_by': 'popularity.desc'
})

# What are the most popular kids movies?

movie = discover.discover_movies({
    'certification_country': 'US',
    'certification.lte': 'G',
    'sort_by': 'popularity.desc'
})
```

Discover TV shows by different types of data like average rating, number
of votes, genres, the network they aired on and air dates.

```python
# What are the most popular TV shows?

show = discover.discover_tv_shows({
    'sort_by': 'popularity.desc'
})

# What are the best dramas?

show = discover.discover_tv_shows({
    'with_genres': 18,
    'sort_by': 'vote_average.desc',
    'vote_count.gte': 10
})
```

Login to your account and add some movie recommendations to your TMDb watchlist.

```python
from tmdbv3api import Account
from tmdbv3api import Authentication
from tmdbv3api import TMDb, Movie

USERNAME = "YOUR_USERNAME"
PASSWORD = "YOUR_PASSWORD"

tmdb = TMDb()
tmdb.api_key = "YOUR_API_KEY"

auth = Authentication(username=USERNAME, password=PASSWORD)

account = Account()
details = account.details()

print("You are logged in as %s. Your account ID is %s." % (details.username, details.id))
print("This session expires at: %s" % auth.expires_at)

movie = Movie()

s = movie.search("Gangs of New York")
first_result = s[0]
recommendations = movie.recommendations(first_result.id)

for recommendation in recommendations:
    print("Adding %s (%s) to watchlist." % (recommendation.title, recommendation.release_date))
    account.add_to_watchlist(details.id, recommendation.id, "movie")
```

## Running Tests

You can run the tests via the command line. You must export your TMDb API key and Session ID as an environment variables.

Your Session ID can be obtained by running `Authentication(username, password).session_id`

### Mac

```bash
$ export TMDB_API_KEY='YOUR_API_KEY'
$ export TMDB_SESSION_ID='YOUR_SESSION_ID'
```

### Windows (Command Prompt)

```bash
$ setx TMDB_API_KEY "YOUR_API_KEY"
$ setx TMDB_SESSION_ID "YOUR_SESSION_ID"
```

### Windows (PowerShell)

```bash
$ $Env:TMDB_API_KEY="YOUR_API_KEY"
$ $Env:TMDB_SESSION_ID="YOUR_SESSION_ID"
```

After you've set up your environmental variables then run:

```bash
$ python -m unittest discover tests/
```

documentation: Create a temporary request token that can be used to validate a TMDb user login.

documentation: This method allows an application to validate a request token by entering a username and password.

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.auth.Authentication.delete_session
documentation: If you would like to delete (or "logout") from a session, call this method with a valid session ID.

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.change.Change.movie_change_list
documentation:
Get the changes for a movie. By default only the last 24 hours are returned.
You can query up to 14 days in a single query by using the start_date and end_date query parameters.
\:param start_date: str
\:param end_date: str
\:param page: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.change.Change.tv_change_list
documentation:
Get a list of all of the TV show ids that have been changed in the past 24 hours.
You can query up to 14 days in a single query by using the start_date and end_date query parameters.
\:param start_date: str
\:param end_date: str
\:param page: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.change.Change.person_change_list

\:param page: int
\:return:

Get the aggregate credits for TV season.
This call differs from the main credits call in that it does not only return the season credits,
but rather is a view of all the cast & crew for all of the episodes belonging to a season.
\:param tv_id: int
\:param season_num: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.movie.Movie.images
documentation:
Get the images that belong to a movie.
Querying images with a language parameter will filter the results.
If you want to include a fallback language (especially useful for backdrops)
you can use the include_image_language parameter.
This should be a comma separated value like so: include_image_language=en,null.
\:param movie_id: int
\:param include_image_language: str
\:return:

\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.person.Person.changes
documentation:
Get the changes for a person. By default only the last 24 hours are returned.
You can query up to 14 days in a single query by using the start_date and end_date query parameters.
\:param person_id: int
\:param start_date: str
\:param end_date: str
\:param page: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.person.Person.movie_credits
documentation:
Get the movie credits for a person.
\:param person_id: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.person.Person.tv_credits
documentation:
Get the TV show credits for a person.
\:param person_id: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.person.Person.combined_credits
documentation:
Get the movie and TV credits together in a single response.
\:param person_id: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.person.Person.external_ids
documentation:
Get the external ids for a person. We currently support the following external sources.
IMDB ID, Facebook, Freebase MID, Freebase ID, Instagram, TVRage ID, and Twitter
\:param person_id: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.person.Person.images
documentation:
Get the images for a person.
\:param person_id: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.person.Person.tagged_images
documentation:
Get the images that this person has been tagged in.
\:param person_id: int
\:param page: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.person.Person.translations
documentation:
Get a list of translations that have been created for a person.
\:param person_id: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.person.Person.latest
documentation:
Get the most newly created person. This is a live response and will continuously change.
:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.person.Person.popular
documentation:
Get the list of popular people on TMDb. This list updates daily.
\:param page: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.person.Person.search
documentation:
Search for people.
\:param term: string
\:param page: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.details
documentation:
Get the primary TV show details by id.
\:param tv_id: int
\:param append_to_response: str
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.account_states
documentation:
Grab the following account states for a session:
TV show rating, If it belongs to your watchlist, or If it belongs to your favourite list.
\:param tv_id: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.aggregate_credits
documentation:
Get the aggregate credits (cast and crew) that have been added to a TV show.
This call differs from the main credits call in that it does not return the newest season but rather,
is a view of all the entire cast & crew for all episodes belonging to a TV show.
\:param tv_id: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.alternative_titles
documentation:
Returns all of the alternative titles for a TV show.
\:param tv_id: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.changes
documentation:
Get the changes for a TV show. By default only the last 24 hours are returned.
You can query up to 14 days in a single query by using the start_date and end_date query parameters.
\:param tv_id: int
\:param start_date: str
\:param end_date: str
\:param page: int

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.content_ratings
documentation:
Get the list of content ratings (certifications) that have been added to a TV show.
\:param tv_id: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.credits
documentation:
Get the credits (cast and crew) that have been added to a TV show.
\:param tv_id: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.episode_groups
documentation:
Get all of the episode groups that have been created for a TV show.
\:param tv_id: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.external_ids
documentation:
Get the external ids for a TV show.
\:param tv_id: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.images
documentation:
Get the images that belong to a TV show.
Querying images with a language parameter will filter the results.
If you want to include a fallback language (especially useful for backdrops)
you can use the include_image_language parameter.
This should be a comma separated value like so: include_image_language=en,null.
\:param tv_id: int
\:param include_image_language: str
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.keywords
documentation:
Get the keywords that have been added to a TV show.
\:param tv_id: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.recommendations
documentation:
Get the list of TV show recommendations for this item.
\:param tv_id: int
\:param page: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.reviews
documentation:
Get the reviews for a TV show.
\:param tv_id: int
\:param page: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.screened_theatrically
documentation:
Get a list of seasons or episodes that have been screened in a film festival or theatre.
\:param tv_id: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.similar
documentation:
Get the primary TV show details by id.
\:param tv_id: int
\:param page: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.translations
documentation:
Get a list of the translations that exist for a TV show.
\:param tv_id: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.videos
documentation:
Get the videos that have been added to a TV show.
\:param tv_id: int
\:param include_video_language: str
\:param page: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.watch_providers
documentation:
You can query this method to get a list of the availabilities per country by provider.
\:param tv_id: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.rate_tv_show
documentation:
Rate a TV show.
\:param tv_id: int
\:param rating: float

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.delete_rating
documentation:
Remove your rating for a TV show.
\:param tv_id: int

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.latest
documentation:
Get the most newly created TV show. This is a live response and will continuously change.
:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.airing_today
documentation:
Get a list of TV shows that are airing today.
This query is purely day based as we do not currently support airing times.
\:param page: int
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.on_the_air
documentation:
Get a list of shows that are currently on the air.
\:param page:
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.popular
documentation:
Get a list of the current popular TV shows on TMDb. This list updates daily.
\:param page:
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.top_rated
documentation:
Get a list of the top rated TV shows on TMDb.
\:param page:
\:return:

methods.name: tmdbv3api.objs.tv.TV.search
documentation:
Search for a TV show.
\:param term:
\:param page:
\:return:
